sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Income Tax Reference Application No. 148 of 2015 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ For hearing of main case 03.10.2025 Mr. Ghazi Khan Khalil, advocate for the applicant Mr. Anwar Kashif Mumtaz, advocate for respondent Mr. Ghazi Khan Khalil, advocate states that the impugned judgment is not a speaking order and has not considered the facts and circumstances relevant to the case. Learned counsel states that despite being the last fact- finding forum neither the evidence has been appreciated nor any independent deliberation is discernible. He states that four page order is some reproduction and conjectures. He states that it may be in the interest of justice and that of all parties for the impugned order to be set aside and matter be remanded back to the Tribunal for adjudication afresh in accordance with law. Learned counsel for respondent articulates no cavil to the aforesaid. Order accordingly. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
